resources.string: No API key found for provider %1$s. Configure one in Settings > API Keys before starting.
Ongoing notification for the ZeroClaw daemon foreground service
Start the daemon automatically after device reboot
Reduce token usage by compressing conversation context
Client-side only - strips thinking tags from console display without affecting daemon behavior
Automatically save conversation context to memory
Wait time before retrying a failed provider
Enforce daily and monthly spending caps
Route outbound traffic through a proxy
Comma-separated service names for selective routing
HTTP requests use a deny-by-default policy. Only domains listed above will be accessible. Leave empty to block all requests.
No allowed domains configured - HTTP requests will be rejected
Let the agent download images from remote URLs for vision
Allow remote image fetch for vision
ISO 639-1 code (e.g. \"en\", \"es\") or blank for auto-detect
Maximum audio clip length to transcribe
Query classification analyses incoming messages to route them to the most appropriate model. No additional configuration is required.
Import failed: wrong passphrase or invalid data
Encrypted storage unavailable. Keys are stored in memory only and will be lost when the app restarts.
Encrypted storage was corrupted and has been reset. Previously stored keys were lost.
%1$d stored key(s) could not be read and may be corrupted.
Enter a passphrase to encrypt your API keys. You will need this passphrase to import them later.
Paste the encrypted export data and enter the passphrase that was used during export.
Delete the %1$s key? This action cannot be undone.
Also delete %1$d agent(s) using this provider
Expected format: number + unit (s, m, h, d)
Enter a positive number of minutes
Track memory usage of sandboxed processes
Require OTP verification for gated actions
Allow emergency shutdown of all agent operations
After triggering e-stop, require OTP to restart
Require OTP to resume after e-stop
ZeroClaw needs a few permissions to run reliably in the background.
Required to show the foreground service notification.
Exempt the app from battery optimization so the daemon is not killed by the system.
Set up a PIN to lock the app on launch and after a period of inactivity.
You can set up app lock later in Settings
ZeroClaw is an AI agent that runs on your device as an always-on service. It connects to messaging platforms like Telegram and Discord, and can be customized with tools, memory, and scheduled tasks.
Select your AI provider and enter credentials. You can add more keys later in Settings.
Review your configuration and start the ZeroClaw daemon.
Control how independently your agent can act.
Agent asks before taking actions. Recommended for most users.
Agent acts within defined boundaries without asking.
Agent acts freely with no restrictions.
Choose messaging platforms for your agent. You can add more later.
You can skip this and add channels later in Settings
ZeroClaw native identity format with extended metadata support.
Configure how your agent remembers conversations.
Fast local database. Best for most users.
Plain text files. Easy to read and edit manually.
No persistent memory. Agent starts fresh each session.
Scanning your network for AI servers...
Make sure a local AI server (Ollama, LM Studio, vLLM, or LocalAI) is running on a device connected to the same network.
"localhost" refers to the phone itself on Android. Enter your computer LAN IP address or use "Scan Network" to find servers automatically.
You can add keys later in Settings
Expose your agent to the internet for webhooks and external access.
Agent accessible only on this device and local network
Tunnel via ngrok for public HTTPS endpoint
Tunnel via Cloudflare for public endpoint
Tunnel via Tailscale for private mesh network access
Specify your own public endpoint URL
You can configure a tunnel later in Settings
Are you sure you want to delete "%1$s"? This cannot be undone.
Display name only - does not change the daemon identity
Hard limit - blocks actions when exceeded
This will remove the channel configuration and all stored credentials.
Tap "Run Diagnostics" to check your configuration.
Create a new bot using Telegram\'s BotFather.
No memory entries. The daemon stores memories as it runs.
No scheduled jobs. Tap + to add a cron job or one-shot task.
Remove the "%1$s" profile for %2$s? This action cannot be undone.
Prevent execution of dangerous shell commands entirely
Agent has unrestricted access to tools and commands.
Agent can only read files and answer questions.
Agent asks for approval on risky actions.
Prompt the user before executing medium-risk actions
Restrict file access to the project workspace directory
Bind the gateway to 0.0.0.0 instead of localhost only
Enforce token-based pairing for gateway connections
Scan QR code to add pairing token
Automatically archive and purge old memory entries
Periodic heartbeat ticks for keep-alive and monitoring
View and edit live running config TOML
This will open the initial setup wizard again. Your agent identity (AIEOS) will be cleared so you can generate a fresh one. API keys and other settings are preserved.
Choose a name for your daemon. This becomes the AIEOS identity and the nickname for your first connection.
You can change the provider and model in connection settings.
Connect a chat platform so your agent can send and receive messages. You can skip this and add channels later in Settings.
You can skip this step and add channels later.
Require PIN on launch and after timeout
Built with ZeroClaw, a Rust-native AI agent framework.
No model usage data available yet.
Route embedding requests to specific providers based on hint keywords.
Paste an AIEOS v1.1 identity JSON document below. This defines your agent\'s personality, name, and capabilities for upstream identity resolution.
Route requests to specific providers based on hint keywords.
Events are written to the daemon log output.
Events are exported to an OpenTelemetry collector.
A future release will add background update checking with configurable frequency and notification preferences.
Automatic update checks are not yet available.
Visit the GitHub releases page to check for newer versions.
%1$s devices may stop the daemon in the background. Disable battery optimization for reliable operation.
Cost summary: %1$s today, %2$s this month. Tap for details.
%1$d active jobs, %2$d paused. Next run: %3$s. Tap for details.
This will immediately halt all agent execution and cancel any active sessions. The daemon will remain running but no actions will be processed.
All agent execution is halted. No actions will be processed until the emergency stop is released.
Emergency stop: halt all agent execution
An API key may be invalid or expired. Check Settings › API Keys.
You switched from %1$s to %2$s. Found %3$d stale %4$s file(s) (%5$s).\n\nDelete old data to prevent conflicts?
Confirm your identity to resume agent execution
This will lift the emergency stop and allow agents to process actions again.
The daemon is running and healthy.
Camera permission required to scan QR codes
Grant camera access to scan QR codes
Camera viewfinder for QR code scanning
Scan QR code to fill API key
%1$s failed to start and were disabled. You can re-enable them in Settings > Connections.
No skills installed. Use install to add skills from a URL.
This app is exempt from battery optimization.
Battery optimization may stop the daemon in the background.
Open your OEM guide to disable aggressive battery restrictions.
This channel type does not support automatic daemon restart. You may need to restart manually after changing this setting.
If you forgot your PIN, reset app data from system settings and set up again.
Too many failed attempts. Try again in %1$d seconds.
No tools available. Start the daemon to see tools.
Create a new bot using BotFather.
Open BotFather using the link below.
Copy the API token BotFather gives you.
You can add multiple IDs separated by commas.
Web Search and Web Fetch have been enabled. You can disable them in Plugins settings.
No test endpoint available for this provider
No valid OAuth credentials found in file
Login timed out — please try again
Authentication failed — check your API key
Access denied — check your API key permissions
Endpoint not found — check the base URL
Provider returned an error — try again later
Connection timed out — check your network
Connection failed — check credentials and URL
The model did not generate a text response.
No chat provider configured — use /help to see admin commands, or add a provider in Settings > API Keys.
No active session — is the daemon running?
Any other input is sent as a chat message.
ZeroClaw Terminal v%1$s — Type /help for commands
ZeroClaw Terminal v%1$s — Admin Console (no chat provider) — Type /help for commands
Invalid API key — verify your credentials before starting the daemon
Show health summary or component health
Show cost for a specific day (defaults to today)
Show cost for a specific month (defaults to current)
Get details of a cron job
List tools provided by a skill
Install a skill from a source
List memories, optionally filtered by category
Bind a user identity to a channel
Swap the active provider and model
List available models for a provider
Schedule a job at a specific time
Schedule a repeating job at an interval
Enter the credentials for this integration.
Configure additional settings for this integration.
Create a new bot using Telegram\'s BotFather.
Open BotFather using the link below.
Copy the API token BotFather gives you.
Keep your bot token secret. Anyone with the token can control your bot.
Restrict which Telegram users can interact with your bot.
Send any message to get your numeric user ID.
Paste your user ID into the field below.
You can add multiple user IDs separated by commas.
Create a new application in the Discord Developer Portal.
Open the Developer Portal using the link below.
Click "New Application" and give it a name.
Go to the Bot section and click "Reset Token".
Enable the Message Content Intent under Privileged Gateway Intents.
Specify which Discord server the bot should operate in.
Enable Developer Mode in Discord settings to copy server and user IDs.
Create a new Slack app with bot and event subscriptions.
Open the Slack App Console using the link below.
Click "Create New App" and choose "From scratch".
Under OAuth & Permissions, add the required bot scopes.
Install the app to your workspace and copy the Bot Token (xoxb-...).
Under Basic Information, generate an App-Level Token (xapp-...) with connections:write scope.
You need both a Bot Token (xoxb-...) and an App Token (xapp-...) for Socket Mode.
Specify which Slack channel the bot should listen in.
Right-click a channel name and select "View channel details" to find the Channel ID at the bottom.
Enter your Matrix homeserver URL and access token.
You can generate an access token from your Matrix client\'s security settings or via the /_matrix/client/v3/login API.
Keep your access token secret. It grants full access to your Matrix account.
Specify the Matrix room and allowed users.
The room ID looks like !abc123:matrix.org. Find it in your client\'s room settings under "Advanced".
Will be verified when daemon starts
Invalid API key — check your credentials
Enable at least one connection to validate API keys
No error events in runtime traces
Self-hosted provider, no API key required
No API key found for provider "%1$s"
API key marked as invalid by provider
OAuth token expired or expiring soon
App is exempt from battery optimization
App is not exempt; service may be killed
No aggressive OEM battery management detected
Notifications disabled; service status won\'t show
Daemon did not become healthy within %1$ss
Channels did not become healthy within %1$ss
Requires billing. Free $5 credit for new accounts
Accepts API keys or OAuth tokens (sk-ant-oat01-...)
Aggregator: access 100+ models with one key. Free tier available
Free tier: 15 req/min for Flash models
URL is optional - defaults to localhost:11434
Start LM Studio\'s local server first, or scan your network
Start your vLLM server first, or scan your network
Start your LocalAI server first, or scan your network
Free tier available for small models
Grok models. Free tier with monthly credits
Uses AWS IAM credentials. Set region in base URL
AI inference with 53+ models via OpenAI-compatible API
Not connected to a local network
An internal error occurred. Please restart the service.
Internal state corrupted. Please restart the app.
Received malformed data from the native layer.
The daemon is not running. Start it from the Dashboard.
Plugin registry URL must use HTTPS.
Plugin registry returned an empty response.
Plugin registry response is too large.
Storage is unavailable or not writable.
Edit the live daemon configuration above. Validation errors (if any) are shown below the editor. Save writes to config_override.toml and restarts the daemon.
Are you sure you want to delete %s?
Use the device file manager to copy files into the workspace directory
Back up or restore app data
Select what to include in the backup. The file is saved to the app\'s private storage.

This will restore data from:
%s

Current data will be replaced.
